name: Red-Line Control
summary: A negotiation discipline that makes the seller's non-negotiable commercial or legal boundaries explicit before the conversation starts.
whenToUseIt: Use red-line control in every meaningful commercial negotiation so the team knows what cannot be traded away because of margin, risk, or precedent.
steps: Define the minimum commercial and legal boundaries internally before negotiation begins.
Align the deal team on what requires escalation and what does not.
Use those lines to protect the deal from reactive concessions under pressure.
example: The AE knows that uncapped liability and a below-floor discount both require executive approval, so those requests are handled calmly instead of improvised live.
